content.problemStatement:
There are NNN candies (numbered 111 through NNN) on a circular table. For each valid iii, candies iii and i+1i+1i+1 are adjacent; candies 111 and NNN are also adjacent. You want to eat all these candies.

Initially, your happiness level is 000. For each valid iii, when you eat the iii-th candy, the happiness level increases by aia_iai​. Note that aia_iai​ may be negative and your happiness level may also become negative.

First, you should eat the candy 111. After that, while there is at least one uneaten candy, you may eat any candy that is adjacent to at least one of the candies eaten so far.

Your goal is to maximise the smallest value of your happiness during this process.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 200,000200,000200,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the maximum possible minimum happiness value during the process.
